# Ren runtime reference

<!-- Code generated by internal/tools/docgen; DO NOT EDIT. -->
<!-- Run `go generate ./...` to regenerate. -->

This is the environment a packaged Risor script runs in: the global builtins available without an import, and the modules importable via the `builtin://` scheme.

## Imports

Scripts load code with the `import` builtin, resolved by URL scheme:

| Form | Resolves to |
|---|---|
| `import("builtin://name")` | A built-in module registered with the runtime (see below). |
| `import("path/to/mod")` | A compiled module from the package, relative to the package root. |
| `import("package://path/to/mod")` | The explicit form of a bare package path. |

## Global builtins

Available to every script without an import.

| Signature | Returns | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `all(items)` | bool | Return true if all elements are truthy<br>Example: `all([true, 1, "yes"])` |
| `any(items)` | bool | Return true if any element is truthy<br>Example: `any([false, 0, "yes"])` |
| `assert(condition, message?)` | nil | Raise an error if condition is false<br>Example: `assert(x > 0, "x must be positive")` |
| `bool(value?)` | bool | Convert value to boolean<br>Example: `bool(1)` |
| `byte(value?)` | byte | Convert value to byte (0-255)<br>Example: `byte(65)` |
| `bytes(value?)` | bytes | Convert value to bytes<br>Example: `bytes("hello")` |
| `call(fn, args...)` | any | Call a function with arguments<br>Example: `call(add, 1, 2)` |
| `chunk(list, size)` | list | Split list into chunks of size n<br>Example: `chunk([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 2)` |
| `coalesce(values...)` | any | Return first non-nil argument<br>Example: `coalesce(nil, nil, "default")` |
| `decode(format, data)` | any | Decode data from a format (json, base64, hex, etc.)<br>Example: `decode("json", '{"a": 1}')` |
| `encode(format, value)` | string | Encode data to a format (json, base64, hex, etc.)<br>Example: `encode("json", {a: 1})` |
| `error(message, args...)` | error | Create an error value (does not throw)<br>Example: `error("file %s not found", name)` |
| `filter(items, fn)` | list | Keep elements where fn returns true<br>Example: `filter([1, 2, 3, 4], x => x > 2)` |
| `float(value?)` | float | Convert value to float<br>Example: `float("3.14")` |
| `getattr(obj, name, default?)` | any | Get attribute from object with optional default<br>Example: `getattr(obj, "name", "unknown")` |
| `import(url)` | module | Load a module and return it; the argument is a package path or a builtin:// URL<br>Example: `import("builtin://os")` |
| `int(value?)` | int | Convert value to integer<br>Example: `int("42")` |
| `keys(container)` | list | Get keys from map or indices from list<br>Example: `keys({a: 1, b: 2})` |
| `len(container)` | int | Return length of container<br>Example: `len([1, 2, 3])` |
| `list(enumerable?)` | list | Convert enumerable to list<br>Example: `list(range(5))` |
| `pack(schema, data)` | bytes | Serialize a map into a little-endian byte buffer according to a schema |
| `packsize(schema)` | int | Return the total byte size of a schema without packing any data |
| `print(value...)` | nil | Write the arguments to standard output separated by spaces and followed by a newline |
| `printf(format, value...)` | nil | Write a formatted string to standard output |
| `range(start_or_stop, stop?, step?)` | range | Generate a sequence of integers<br>Example: `range(1, 10, 2)` |
| `reversed(sequence)` | list\|string | Return reversed copy of list or string<br>Example: `reversed([1, 2, 3])` |
| `sorted(items, key?)` | list | Return sorted copy of list<br>Example: `sorted([3, 1, 2])` |
| `sprintf(format, args...)` | string | Format string with arguments<br>Example: `sprintf("%s: %d", "count", 42)` |
| `string(value?)` | string | Convert value to string<br>Example: `string(123)` |
| `type(value)` | string | Return type name of value<br>Example: `type([1, 2, 3])` |
| `unpack(schema, buffer)` | map | Deserialize a little-endian byte buffer into a map according to a schema |

## Modules

Import with `import("builtin://<name>")`, for example `os := import("builtin://os")`.

### `os`

Process, environment, and user/group information.

| Signature | Returns | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `args()` | list | Return the script's command-line arguments |
| `chdir(dir)` | nil | Change the script's working directory |
| `current_user()` | map | Return the current user as a map of its fields |
| `environ()` | list | Return the environment as a list of "key=value" strings |
| `exit(code)` | nil | Exit the script with the given status code |
| `getenv(key)` | string | Return the value of an environment variable, or an empty string if unset |
| `getpid()` | int | Return the process ID of the caller |
| `getuid()` | int | Return the numeric user ID of the caller |
| `getwd()` | string | Return the script's current working directory |
| `hostname()` | string | Return the host name reported by the kernel |
| `lookup_gid(gid)` | map | Look up a group by numeric ID |
| `lookup_group(name)` | map | Look up a group by name |
| `lookup_uid(uid)` | map | Look up a user by numeric ID |
| `lookup_user(username)` | map | Look up a user by username |
| `setenv(key, value)` | nil | Set an environment variable |
| `stdin()` | file | The standard input stream as a file object |
| `stdout()` | file | The standard output stream as a file object |
| `temp_dir()` | string | Return the default directory for temporary files |
| `unsetenv(key)` | nil | Remove an environment variable |
| `user_cache_dir()` | string | Return the default root directory for user-specific cached data |
| `user_config_dir()` | string | Return the default root directory for user-specific configuration |
| `user_home_dir()` | string | Return the current user's home directory |

### `fs`

Filesystem operations over the scheme-based virtual filesystems registered with the runtime.

| Signature | Returns | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `err_closed()` | error | Error sentinel: the file is already closed |
| `err_exist()` | error | Error sentinel: the file already exists |
| `err_invalid()` | error | Error sentinel: invalid argument |
| `err_not_exist()` | error | Error sentinel: the file does not exist |
| `err_permission()` | error | Error sentinel: permission denied |
| `mkdir(path, perm)` | nil | Create a single directory |
| `mkdir_all(path, perm)` | nil | Create a directory along with any missing parents |
| `mkdir_temp(dir, pattern)` | string | Create a new temporary directory and return its path |
| `open_file(path, mode, perm)` | file | Open a file and return a file object; mode is a fopen-style string such as "r", "w", or "a+" |
| `read_dir(path)` | list | List a directory and return its entries |
| `read_file(path)` | bytes | Read a file and return its contents |
| `remove(path)` | nil | Delete a file or empty directory |
| `remove_all(path)` | nil | Delete a path and any children it contains |
| `rename(oldpath, newpath)` | nil | Move a file or directory (cannot cross filesystem boundaries) |
| `stat(path)` | file_info | Return a file_info object describing a file |
| `symlink(oldname, newname)` | nil | Create a symbolic link (cannot cross filesystem boundaries) |
| `write_file(path, data, perm)` | nil | Write data to a file, creating it as needed |

### `filepath`

URL-aware path manipulation helpers.

| Signature | Returns | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `abs(path, wd)` | string | Return the absolute form of a path resolved against a working directory |
| `base(path)` | string | Return the last element of a path |
| `clean(path)` | string | Return the shortest equivalent form of a path |
| `dir(path)` | string | Return all but the last element of a path |
| `ext(path)` | string | Return the file name extension of a path |
| `is_abs(path)` | bool | Report whether a path is absolute |
| `join(elem...)` | string | Join any number of path elements into a single cleaned path |
| `match(pattern, name)` | bool | Report whether a name matches a shell pattern |
| `split(path)` | list | Split a path into directory and file components |

### `shlex`

Split command-line strings into arguments using shell-style quoting.

| Signature | Returns | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `argv(line)` | list | Split a command-line string into a list of arguments following shell-style quoting and escaping |

### `dll`

Load dynamic-link libraries and call their exported procedures (Windows only).

| Signature | Returns | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `load(path)` | handle | Open the dynamic-link library at the given path and return a handle (Windows only; fails on other platforms) |
